title: Variable Greeting
description:
Create a variable called 'language' with the value 'Python', and a variable called 'version' with the value 3. Then use an f-string to print:

My language is Python and its version is 3.
starter_code:
# Create your variables here

# Use print() with an f-string
expected_output: My language is Python and its version is 3.

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'language' set to 'Python'
code_requirements.fail_message: Make sure you create a variable called 'language' set to 'Python'.

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'version' set to 3
code_requirements.fail_message: Make sure you create a variable called 'version' set to 3.

code_requirements.description: Uses an f-string to format the output
code_requirements.fail_message: Use an f-string (e.g. f'...') to build the output, not a plain hardcoded string.

hints: Use language = 'Python' to store the string value
Use version = 3 to store the number (no quotes needed)
Use f'My language is {language} and its version is {version}.' for the output

tests.expected_output: My language is Python and its version is 3.
